findings.description: Python command on line 101 uses unquoted $SKILL_FILE variable; if the variable evaluates to empty or contains special characters, the Python invocation could fail. However, $SKILL_FILE is derived from git rev-parse output (set at line 56), making this extremely low risk in practice. No immediate fix required, but consider defensive quoting ('"$SKILL_FILE"') for robustness.

summary: New structural boundary test for sprint SC coverage escalation. REVIEW-DEFENSE comment accurately explains bash variable expansion in the double-quoted python3 invocation; the defense is verified and correct. Tests use appropriate grep/Python patterns for boundary verification. Minor maintainability note on variable quoting. security_overlay_warranted: no, performance_overlay_warranted: no
